concerns.text: FR-009 mandates validation by 'switching from Python to TypeScript', but User Story 1 (US-1) and Acceptance Scenario 3 explicitly define the held-out validation set as 'Java repositories'. This creates a terminology and scope conflict regarding the specific language used for cross-validation.

FR-001 states the system must download repositories 'from the HuggingFace Datasets repository', but the Assumptions section and Idea Md refer to the specific dataset 'codeparrot/github-code'. While likely the same source, the FR uses a generic platform description while the rest of the spec uses a specific dataset name, creating minor terminological inconsistency.
